function.arguments:
{"title": "OS Model: TUI and OneShot Interface Adaptation", "dependencies": "#6", "branch_name": "gh-os-architecture", "is_final": "false", "body": "## Overview

Adapt the TUI and OneShot interfaces to work with the new OS model through the compatibility layer. This ensures existing user interfaces continue to work during the migration.

## Background

Current interfaces:
- **OneShot**: Single conversation execution, batch mode
- **TUI**: Interactive multi-agent terminal UI

Both currently use `Runtime` directly. We need to:
1. Make them work with the compatibility layer
2. Eventually refactor to use OS natively for better features

## TUI Adaptation

### Current Structure
```haskell
-- Current TUI uses Runtime
data TuiAgent = TuiAgent
  { tuiAgent :: Agent (LlmTurnContent, Session)
  , tuiTree :: AgentTree
  }

data Core = Core
  { coreAgents :: [TuiAgent]
  , coreConversations :: TVar [Conversation]
  }
```

### Adapted Structure
```haskell
-- New TUI uses OS through compatibility layer
data TuiAgent = TuiAgent
  { tuiAgentId :: AgentId
  , tuiBridge :: RuntimeBridge
  , tuiTree :: AgentTree
  }

data Core = Core
  { coreOS :: OS  -- Shared OS instance
  , coreAgents :: [TuiAgent]
  , coreConversations :: TVar [Conversation]
  }
```

### Multi-Agent Coordination
```haskell
-- Enable multi-agent conversations in TUI
newtype MultiAgentConfig = MultiAgentConfig
  { maAgents :: [AgentRoleConfig]
  , maCoordinationStrategy :: CoordinationStrategy
  } deriving (Show)

data AgentRoleConfig = AgentRoleConfig
  { arcAgentId :: AgentId
  , arcRole :: AgentRole
  , arcCanInitiate :: Bool
  , arcSubscribesTo :: [AgentId]
  } deriving (Show)

newtype AgentBus = AgentBus
  { busChannels :: TVar (Map AgentId (TQueue InterAgentMessage))
  }

data InterAgentMessage = InterAgentMessage
  { iamFrom :: AgentId
  , iamTo :: AgentId
  , iamType :: MessageType
  , iamContent :: Value
  } deriving (Show)
```

### Layout Modes
```haskell
data LayoutMode
  = SingleAgent
  | SplitVertical
  | SplitHorizontal
  | GridLayout Int Int
  | Tabbed
  deriving (Show, Eq)

newtype TUIConfig = TUIConfig
  { tuiTheme :: Theme
  , tuiKeyBindings :: Map Key EventType
  , tuiLayout :: LayoutMode
  } deriving (Show)
```

## OneShot Adaptation

### Current OneShot
```haskell
mainOneShotText :: SessionStore -> Maybe FilePath -> Maybe Session -> Props -> Text -> IO ()
runtimeToAgent :: SessionStore -> Maybe FilePath -> ConversationId -> Runtime -> IO (Agent (LlmTurnContent, Session))
```

### Adapted OneShot
```haskell
-- Use OS through compatibility layer
mainOneShotText :: SessionStore -> Maybe FilePath -> Maybe Session -> Props -> Text -> IO ()
-- Implementation uses RuntimeBridge internally

-- Future: Native OS version
mainOneShotTextOS :: OS -> Maybe FilePath -> Maybe Session -> AgentId -> Text -> OSM Text
```

## Interface Abstraction

```haskell
-- Common interface for both TUI and OneShot
class OSInterface m where
  initInterface :: OS -> m InterfaceHandle
  runInterface :: InterfaceHandle -> m ()
  shutdownInterface :: InterfaceHandle -> m ()

newtype InterfaceHandle = InterfaceHandle
  { ihOS :: OS
  , ihEventSub :: TQueue OSEvent
  , ihShutdown :: TVar Bool
  }
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Update `System.Agents.TUI.Core` to use `RuntimeBridge`
- [ ] Update `System.Agents.TUI.Types` to reference `AgentId` instead of `Agent`
- [ ] Update `System.Agents.OneShot` to use `RuntimeBridge`
- [ ] Ensure TUI can run multiple agents with shared OS
- [ ] Ensure OneShot works with compatibility layer
- [ ] Add TUI configuration for layout modes
- [ ] Add agent bus for inter-agent communication (foundation)
- [ ] Write integration tests for TUI
- [ ] Write integration tests for OneShot

## Tradeoffs

1. **Complexity vs Features**: Multi-agent TUI adds significant complexity. Start with basic support.

2. **Shared State**: TUI agents now share an OS, enabling shared toolboxes and resources.

3. **Migration Path**: Keep TUI changes minimal in this phase. Major refactoring comes later.

## Integration Points

Uses types from:
- `System.Agents.OS.Compat.Runtime` (RuntimeBridge, AgentRuntime)
- `System.Agents.OS` (OS, OSEvent, AgentId)
- Existing: `System.Agents.TUI.*` modules
- Existing: `System.Agents.OneShot`

## Files to Create/Modify

- Modify: `src/System/Agents/TUI/Core.hs` (use RuntimeBridge)
- Modify: `src/System/Agents/TUI/Types.hs` (update TuiAgent)
- Modify: `src/System/Agents/OneShot.hs` (use RuntimeBridge)
- Create: `src/System/Agents/OS/Interfaces.hs` (common interface)
- Create: `src/System/Agents/OS/Interfaces/TUI.hs` (TUI-specific)
- Create: `src/System/Agents/OS/Interfaces/OneShot.hs` (OneShot-specific)
- Create: `test/OS/InterfaceTests.hs`"}

function.arguments:
{"title": "OS Model: Persistence Layer for Sessions and Tool Calls", "dependencies": "#5", "branch_name": "gh-os-architecture", "is_final": "false", "body": "## Overview

Implement a persistence layer for the OS model that can store sessions, tool calls, and entity states in an RDBMS. This enables durable state and querying across restarts.

## Background

Current `SessionStore` only persists session files. We need:
- Durable storage for all OS state (agents, toolboxes, conversations, turns, tool calls)
- Query capabilities for analytics and debugging
- Migration support from file-based to RDBMS storage

## Types to Define

### Persistence Interface
```haskell
class Monad m => Persistence m where
  persistEntity :: Component a => EntityId -> a -> m ()
  loadEntity :: Component a => EntityId -> m (Maybe a)
  queryEntities :: Component a => EntityQuery a -> m [EntityId]
  deleteEntity :: EntityId -> m ()
  persistEvent :: OSEvent -> m ()

data EntityQuery a = EntityQuery
  { eqFilter :: Maybe (a -> Bool)
  , eqLimit :: Maybe Int
  , eqOrder :: Maybe (a -> a -> Ordering)
  }
```

### Persistence Backend
```haskell
data PersistenceBackend
  = FileBackend FilePersistence
  | SqliteBackend SqlitePersistence
  | PostgresBackend PostgresPersistence

data FilePersistence = FilePersistence
  { fpBasePath :: FilePath
  , fpFormat :: FileFormat
  }

data FileFormat = JsonFormat | BinaryFormat

data SqlitePersistence = SqlitePersistence
  { spConnectionPool :: Pool Connection
  , spSchemaVersion :: Int
  }

data PostgresPersistence = PostgresPersistence
  { pgConnectionPool :: Pool Connection
  , pgSchemaVersion :: Int
  }
```

### Persistable Components
```haskell
-- | Marker class for components that can be persisted
class (Component a, ToJSON a, FromJSON a) => Persistable a where
  persistenceTable :: Proxy a -> Text
  persistenceKey :: Proxy a -> EntityId -> Text

-- Instance for key components
instance Persistable AgentConfig where
  persistenceTable _ = "agents"
  persistenceKey _ eid = "agent:" <> entityIdToText eid

instance Persistable ConversationConfig where
  persistenceTable _ = "conversations"
  persistenceKey _ eid = "conv:" <> entityIdToText eid

instance Persistable Message where
  persistenceTable _ = "messages"
  persistenceKey _ eid = "msg:" <> entityIdToText eid

-- ... similar for other persistable components
```

### Persistence Events
```haskell
data PersistenceEvent
  = EntityPersisted EntityId ComponentTypeId
  | EntityLoaded EntityId (Maybe ComponentTypeId)
  | EntityDeleted EntityId
  | BatchPersistCompleted Int  -- Count of entities
  | PersistenceError Text
  deriving (Show)
```

## Database Schema (SQLite/Postgres)

```sql
-- Entities table (sparse representation)
CREATE TABLE entities (
  entity_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  entity_type TEXT NOT NULL,  -- agent, toolbox, conversation, etc.
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

-- Components table (JSON storage for flexibility)
CREATE TABLE components (
  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
  entity_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES entities(entity_id),
  component_type INTEGER NOT NULL,
  component_data JSON NOT NULL,
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  UNIQUE(entity_id, component_type)
);

-- Events table (append-only log)
CREATE TABLE events (
  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
  event_type TEXT NOT NULL,
  event_data JSON NOT NULL,
  entity_id TEXT REFERENCES entities(entity_id),
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

-- Messages table (optimized for conversation queries)
CREATE TABLE messages (
  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
  conversation_id TEXT NOT NULL,
  turn_id TEXT NOT NULL,
  role TEXT NOT NULL,
  content TEXT NOT NULL,
  tool_calls JSON,
  timestamp TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

-- Tool calls table (lineage tracking)
CREATE TABLE tool_calls (
  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
  tool_call_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
  turn_id TEXT NOT NULL,
  tool_name TEXT NOT NULL,
  tool_input JSON NOT NULL,
  tool_result JSON,
  parent_call_id TEXT,
  started_at TIMESTAMP,
  completed_at TIMESTAMP,
  status TEXT NOT NULL
);

-- Indexes
CREATE INDEX idx_components_entity ON components(entity_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_components_type ON components(component_type);
CREATE INDEX idx_events_entity ON events(entity_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_messages_conversation ON messages(conversation_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_messages_turn ON messages(turn_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_tool_calls_turn ON tool_calls(turn_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_tool_calls_parent ON tool_calls(parent_call_id);
```

## Persistence Configuration
```haskell
data PersistenceConfig = PersistenceConfig
  { persistenceBackend :: PersistenceBackendType
  , persistenceSyncInterval :: NominalDiffTime
  , persistenceBatchSize :: Int
  }

data PersistenceBackendType
  = InMemory  -- No persistence
  | FileBackendType FilePath
  | SqliteBackendType FilePath
  | PostgresBackendType ConnectionString
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Create `System.Agents.OS.Persistence` module
- [ ] Define `Persistence` typeclass
- [ ] Implement `Persistable` for key components (AgentConfig, ConversationConfig, Message, etc.)
- [ ] Implement SQLite backend with schema creation
- [ ] Implement file-based backend for compatibility
- [ ] Add migration support from file-based storage
- [ ] Write tests for persistence operations
- [ ] Write tests for data migration
- [ ] Add query examples for common use cases
- [ ] Document performance characteristics

## Tradeoffs

1. **JSON vs Normalized**: JSON storage in components table provides flexibility but loses queryability. Consider partial normalization for frequently-queried fields.

2. **Sync vs Async**: Synchronous persistence is simpler but may slow down the OS. Consider async with durability guarantees.

3. **Single vs Multi-Backend**: Supporting multiple backends adds complexity. Start with SQLite, add Postgres later.

## Integration Points

Uses types from:
- `System.Agents.OS` (OS, OSM, EntityId, OSEvent)
- `System.Agents.OS.Core` (Component)
- `System.Agents.OS.Conversation` (Message, ConversationConfig)

Replaces/extends:
- `System.Agents.SessionStore`

## Files to Create/Modify

- Create: `src/System/Agents/OS/Persistence.hs`
- Create: `src/System/Agents/OS/Persistence/Types.hs`
- Create: `src/System/Agents/OS/Persistence/Sqlite.hs`
- Create: `src/System/Agents/OS/Persistence/File.hs`
- Create: `src/System/Agents/OS/Persistence/Schema.hs`
- Modify: `src/System/Agents/SessionStore.hs` (deprecate, delegate to new)
- Create: `test/OS/PersistenceTests.hs`"}

contents.userPrompt:
You are an expert Haskell programmer specialized in analyzing user requirements and Haskell code.
You prepare well-documented individual issues.
You should chunk the work into smaller issues following the 'Feature Branch Isolation' model:
- Identify a 'Feature Branch' name (e.g., 'gh-123' for issue #123).
- All sub-tasks for this feature must use this branch name as their 'branch_name' argument.
- All sub-tasks should pull from and merge to this feature branch.
- Enforce linearization of work by setting 'dependencies' appropriately (e.g., task B depends on task A).
- Add a final task (the 'ultimate PR') that merges the feature branch into 'main'. This task must have 'is_final' set to 'true'.
Each individual issue you create will be executed by a human of software agent independently from each other and in the order specified by dependencies.
Your strategy should be as follows:
- avoid chunks that will generate conflicts (i.e., individual issues should not ask to modify the same file unless they are sequential)
- repeat important data types between different tasks for a same project when they are used in multiple files
You mention important tradeoffs.
You can ask user questions, when doing so always assume the answer could be 'i do not know'.
Do not create tracking issues.
You can call yourself recursively using the architect tool, however each recursive call will incur delay and cost, thus limit these calls to tasks that are worthwile of chunking (e.g., reading and analyzing some web page or document).
The documenter agent can help you navigate the knowledge base.
Your output format may be stored as commit message: hence the first line of your last message should be a summary line.

{"title": "OS Model: Core Entity and Component Types", "dependencies": "", "branch_name": "gh-os-architecture", "is_final": "false", "body": "## Overview

Define the foundational Entity-Component-System (ECS) types for the new Operating System architecture. This is the foundational issue that establishes the core data model inspired by https://dicioccio.fr/how-i-write-purescript-apps.html#the-cheap-entity-component-system.

## Background

Moving from a hierarchical Runtime-per-agent structure to a flat ECS model requires redefining how we represent agents, toolboxes, and resources. In ECS:
- **Entities** are unique identifiers (just IDs)
- **Components** are pure data attached to entities
- **Systems** are functions that process entities with specific components

## Types to Define

### Core Identifiers
```haskell
-- | Unique identifier for any entity in the system
newtype EntityId = EntityId { unEntityId :: UUID }
  deriving (Eq, Ord, Show, Hashable)

-- | Phantom-typed entity IDs for type safety
newtype AgentId = AgentId { unAgentId :: EntityId }
newtype ToolboxId = ToolboxId { unToolboxId :: EntityId }
newtype ConversationId = ConversationId { unConversationId :: EntityId }
newtype TurnId = TurnId { unTurnId :: EntityId }
newtype ToolCallId = ToolCallId { unToolCallId :: EntityId }
newtype ResourceId = ResourceId { unResourceId :: EntityId }
```

### Component System
```haskell
-- | A component is any data type that can be attached to an entity
class Component a where
  componentId :: Proxy a -> ComponentTypeId

-- | Type-level identifier for component types
newtype ComponentTypeId = ComponentTypeId { unComponentTypeId :: Int }
  deriving (Eq, Ord, Show)

-- | Storage for all components of a specific type
newtype ComponentStore a = ComponentStore
  { store :: HashMap EntityId a }

-- | The world contains all component stores
newtype World = World
  { componentStores :: HashMap ComponentTypeId (TVar Any) }
```

### Agent Components
```haskell
newtype AgentConfig = AgentConfig
  { agentName :: Text
  , agentModel :: ModelConfig
  , agentSystemPrompt :: Text
  , agentToolboxBindings :: [ToolboxBindingSpec]
  } deriving (Show, Generic)

instance Component AgentConfig where
  componentId _ = ComponentTypeId 1

newtype AgentState = AgentState
  { agentStatus :: AgentStatus
  , agentCurrentConversation :: Maybe ConversationId
  , agentCreatedAt :: UTCTime
  } deriving (Show)

instance Component AgentState where
  componentId _ = ComponentTypeId 2

data AgentStatus = AgentIdle | AgentBusy TurnId | AgentError Text
  deriving (Show, Eq)
```

### Toolbox Components
```haskell
newtype ToolboxConfig = ToolboxConfig
  { toolboxName :: Text
  , toolboxType :: ToolboxType
  , toolboxSettings :: Value
  } deriving (Show)

instance Component ToolboxConfig where
  componentId _ = ComponentTypeId 3

newtype ToolboxState = ToolboxState
  { toolboxScope :: ResourceScope
  , toolboxStatus :: ToolboxStatus
  , toolboxResourceRef :: Maybe ResourceId
  } deriving (Show)

instance Component ToolboxState where
  componentId _ = ComponentTypeId 4

newtype ToolboxBinding = ToolboxBinding
  { bindingAgentId :: AgentId
  , bindingToolboxId :: ToolboxId
  , bindingConfig :: Value
  } deriving (Show)

instance Component ToolboxBinding where
  componentId _ = ComponentTypeId 5
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Create new module `System.Agents.OS.Core`
- [ ] Define `EntityId` and phantom-typed ID wrappers
- [ ] Define `Component` typeclass with auto-generated IDs
- [ ] Define `World` type with heterogeneous component storage
- [ ] Define all agent-related components
- [ ] Define all toolbox-related components
- [ ] Add type safety tests to verify phantom types work correctly
- [ ] Document tradeoffs in module header

## Tradeoffs

1. **Type Erasure (Any) vs Type Safety**: Using `TVar Any` requires careful casting but enables heterogeneous storage. Alternative would be a closed-world sum type which limits extensibility.

2. **STM vs MVar**: STM allows composable transactions, preferred for complex resource coordination across multiple entities.

3. **Component ID Assignment**: Consider Template Haskell for auto-generating IDs to prevent collisions when adding new component types.

## Migration Notes

This module is foundational and does not interact with existing code. It establishes the new data model that will eventually replace:
- `System.Agents.Runtime.Runtime`
- `System.Agents.Base.Agent` (description part)
- Parts of `System.Agents.Tools.Base`

## Files to Create/Modify

- Create: `src/System/Agents/OS/Core.hs`
- Create: `src/System/Agents/OS/Core/Types.hs`
- Create: `src/System/Agents/OS/Core/World.hs`
- Create: `test/OS/CoreTests.hs`"}

{"title": "OS Model: Resource Management and Lifecycle Scopes", "dependencies": "#1", "branch_name": "gh-os-architecture", "is_final": "false", "body": "## Overview

Define the resource management system with multiple lifecycle scopes and proper cleanup. This issue depends on the Core Entity types being defined first.

## Background

Tools require resources like:
- **SQLite**: file handles, prepared statements
- **Lua**: interpreter states
- **HTTP**: connection managers
- **Processes**: file descriptors

These resources have different lifecycles:
- **per-program**: Global HTTP connection pool
- **per-agent**: Agent-specific sandbox
- **per-toolbox**: SQLite connection per toolbox instance
- **per-conversation**: Isolated Lua state
- **per-turn**: Temporary resources
- **per-toolcall**: Single-use resources

## Types to Define

### Scope Definition
```haskell
-- | Defines the lifetime of a resource
newtype ResourceScope = ResourceScope
  { scopeHierarchy :: [ScopeLevel] }

data ScopeLevel
  = ProgramScope
  | AgentScope AgentId
  | ToolboxScope ToolboxId
  | ConversationScope ConversationId
  | TurnScope TurnId
  | ToolCallScope ToolCallId
  deriving (Eq, Ord, Show)
```

### Resource Components
```haskell
newtype ResourceInfo = ResourceInfo
  { resourceId :: ResourceId
  , resourceScope :: ResourceScope
  , resourceType :: ResourceType
  , resourceCreatedAt :: UTCTime
  , resourceLastAccessed :: TVar UTCTime
  } deriving (Show)

instance Component ResourceInfo where
  componentId _ = ComponentTypeId 10

data ResourceType
  = SqliteResource SqliteConfig
  | LuaResource LuaConfig
  | HttpManagerResource HttpManagerConfig
  | ProcessResource ProcessConfig
  deriving (Show)

data ResourceHandle = ResourceHandle
  { handleId :: ResourceId
  , handleCleanup :: IO ()
  , handleAccess :: forall a. (ResourceAccessor -> IO a) -> IO a
  }

newtype ResourceRegistry = ResourceRegistry
  { registryHandles :: TVar (HashMap ResourceId ResourceHandle)
  , registryCounter :: TVar Int
  }

-- Context for resource operations
newtype ResourceContext = ResourceContext
  { contextScope :: [ScopeLevel]
  , contextRegistry :: ResourceRegistry
  }
```

### Resource-Specific Components
```haskell
newtype SqliteResourceData = SqliteResourceData
  { sqliteConnection :: Connection
  , sqliteAccessMode :: SqliteAccessMode
  } deriving (Show)

instance Component SqliteResourceData where
  componentId _ = ComponentTypeId 11

data SqliteAccessMode = SerialAccess | ConcurrentReads
  deriving (Show, Eq)

newtype LuaResourceData = LuaResourceData
  { luaState :: Lua.State
  , luaSandboxed :: Bool
  } deriving (Show)

instance Component LuaResourceData where
  componentId _ = ComponentTypeId 12

newtype HttpResourceData = HttpResourceData
  { httpManager :: Manager
  , httpConnectionCount :: TVar Int
  } deriving (Show)

instance Component HttpResourceData where
  componentId _ = ComponentTypeId 13
```

### Operations
```haskell
createResource :: ResourceContext -> ResourceType 
               -> (ResourceId -> IO ResourceHandle) -> IO ResourceId
cleanupScope :: ResourceRegistry -> ScopeLevel -> IO ()
isResourceValid :: ResourceScope -> [ScopeLevel] -> Bool
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Create new module `System.Agents.OS.Resources`
- [ ] Define `ResourceScope` with all lifecycle levels
- [ ] Define `ResourceRegistry` for tracking all resources
- [ ] Implement `createResource` with proper scope tracking
- [ ] Implement `cleanupScope` that properly disposes all resources in a scope
- [ ] Add resource-specific components for SQLite, Lua, HTTP
- [ ] Write tests for resource lifecycle management
- [ ] Write tests for scope cleanup
- [ ] Document cleanup ordering guarantees

## Tradeoffs

1. **Explicit Cleanup vs Finalizers**: Explicit cleanup (`handleCleanup`) is used for predictable resource management. Finalizers are non-deterministic.

2. **Resource Sharing**: Shared resources use the same ResourceId. The registry tracks implicitly via cleanup - if two entities share a resource, only one should register cleanup.

3. **Scope Checking**: Runtime scope checking adds overhead but ensures correctness. Consider caching scope lookups.

## Integration Points

Uses types from:
- `System.Agents.OS.Core` (EntityId, Component, World)
- Existing: `System.Agents.Tools.SqliteToolbox` (for Connection type)
- Existing: `System.Agents.Tools.LuaToolbox` (for Lua.State type)

## Files to Create/Modify

- Create: `src/System/Agents/OS/Resources.hs`
- Create: `src/System/Agents/OS/Resources/Types.hs`
- Create: `src/System/Agents/OS/Resources/Sqlite.hs`
- Create: `src/System/Agents/OS/Resources/Lua.hs`
- Create: `src/System/Agents/OS/Resources/Http.hs`
- Create: `test/OS/ResourcesTests.hs`"}

function.arguments:
{"title": "OS Model: Concurrent Access and Serialization", "dependencies": "#2", "branch_name": "gh-os-architecture", "is_final": "false", "body": "## Overview

Design concurrent access patterns for shared resources. This builds on the Resource Management system to provide safe concurrent access to toolbox resources.

## Background

Multiple agents may need concurrent access to:
- Same SQLite database (readers can be concurrent, writers must be exclusive)
- HTTP connection pools (can be shared across concurrent requests)
- Lua interpreters (typically exclusive access per execution)
- Process handles (exclusive per process)

The current implementation uses MVars within each toolbox. The new system should use STM for composability and support multiple access patterns.

## Types to Define

### Access Patterns
```haskell
-- | Access pattern for a resource
data AccessPattern
  = ExclusiveAccess      -- Only one accessor (MVar-like)
  | ReadWriteAccess      -- Multiple readers, single writer
  | PoolAccess Int       -- Connection pool with max size
  | StatelessAccess      -- No synchronization needed

-- | Resource access control configuration
newtype AccessControl = AccessControl
  { accessPattern :: AccessPattern
  , accessTimeout :: Maybe NominalDiffTime
  } deriving (Show)

instance Component AccessControl where
  componentId _ = ComponentTypeId 20
```

### Synchronization Primitives
```haskell
-- STM-based read-write lock
data RWLock = RWLock
  { rwLockReaders :: TVar Int
  , rwLockWriter :: TVar Bool
  , rwLockWaitQueue :: TVar [TMVar ()]
  }

-- Resource synchronization state
newtype ResourceSync = ResourceSync
  { syncLock :: SyncPrimitive
  } deriving (Show)

instance Component ResourceSync where
  componentId _ = ComponentTypeId 21

data SyncPrimitive
  = ExclusiveLock (TMVar ())
  | ReadWriteLock RWLock
  | PoolLock (TBQueue ResourceToken)
  | NoLock
  deriving (Show)

data ResourceToken = ResourceToken
  { tokenResource :: ResourceId
  , tokenRelease :: STM ()
  }
```

### Resource Access Monad
```haskell
newtype ResourceM a = ResourceM
  { unResourceM :: ReaderT ResourceContext (ExceptT ResourceError IO) a }

data ResourceError
  = ResourceNotFound ResourceId
  | ResourceAccessTimeout ResourceId NominalDiffTime
  | ResourceScopeInvalid ResourceId [ScopeLevel]
  | ResourceClosed ResourceId
  | ResourceBusy ResourceId
  deriving (Show, Eq)

-- Operations
withExclusive :: ResourceId -> ResourceM a -> ResourceM a
withRead :: ResourceId -> ResourceM a -> ResourceM a
withWrite :: ResourceId -> ResourceM a -> ResourceM a
withPooled :: ResourceId -> ResourceM a -> ResourceM a
```

### Resource-Specific Access Control
```haskell
newtype SqliteAccess = SqliteAccess
  { sqliteRwLock :: RWLock
  , sqliteWalMode :: Bool
  } deriving (Show)

instance Component SqliteAccess where
  componentId _ = ComponentTypeId 22

newtype LuaAccess = LuaAccess
  { luaLock :: TMVar () } deriving (Show)

instance Component LuaAccess where
  componentId _ = ComponentTypeId 23

newtype HttpAccess = HttpAccess
  { httpPool :: TBQueue ResourceToken
  , httpManagerRef :: ResourceId
  } deriving (Show)

instance Component HttpAccess where
  componentId _ = ComponentTypeId 24
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Create new module `System.Agents.OS.Concurrent`
- [ ] Implement STM-based RWLock with reader/writer semantics
- [ ] Implement `withExclusive` using TMVar
- [ ] Implement `withRead`/`withWrite` using RWLock
- [ ] Implement `withPooled` using TBQueue
- [ ] Add resource-specific access components for SQLite, Lua, HTTP
- [ ] Write concurrency tests with multiple threads
- [ ] Write stress tests for resource contention
- [ ] Document thread-safety guarantees

## Tradeoffs

1. **STM vs MVar**: STM chosen for composable transactions and automatic retry on conflict. MVar is slightly faster but less composable.

2. **Fairness**: Current RWLock design can starve writers. Consider writer-priority variant if contention becomes an issue.

3. **Pool Sizing**: Static pool sizes are simpler but may waste resources. Dynamic sizing could be added later.

4. **Timeout Handling**: Optional timeouts allow callers to decide between waiting and failing fast.

## Integration Points

Uses types from:
- `System.Agents.OS.Core` (EntityId, Component)
- `System.Agents.OS.Resources` (ResourceId, ResourceContext, ResourceRegistry)

## Files to Create/Modify

- Create: `src/System/Agents/OS/Concurrent.hs`
- Create: `src/System/Agents/OS/Concurrent/Types.hs`
- Create: `src/System/Agents/OS/Concurrent/Locks.hs`
- Create: `test/OS/ConcurrentTests.hs`"}

{"title": "OS Model: Conversation and Lineage Tracking", "dependencies": "#1", "branch_name": "gh-os-architecture", "is_final": "false", "body": "## Overview

Define types for conversation/thread tracking and lineage tracking for nested tool calls. This enables thorough tracing of agent interactions and tool call hierarchies.

## Background

The system needs to track:
- **Conversations**: Top-level interactions between users and agents
- **Turns**: Individual steps within a conversation
- **Tool Calls**: Individual tool invocations, potentially nested
- **Lineage**: Full call chain for debugging and accounting

This supports:
- Recursive/nested agent calls with proper depth tracking
- Conversation forking (exploring multiple responses)
- Complete audit trails for debugging

## Types to Define

### Conversation Components
```haskell
newtype ConversationConfig = ConversationConfig
  { conversationTitle :: Maybe Text
  , conversationMetadata :: Map Text Value
  } deriving (Show)

instance Component ConversationConfig where
  componentId _ = ComponentTypeId 30

newtype ConversationState = ConversationState
  { conversationAgentId :: AgentId
  , conversationStatus :: ConversationStatus
  , conversationStartedAt :: UTCTime
  , conversationLastActivity :: TVar UTCTime
  } deriving (Show)

instance Component ConversationState where
  componentId _ = ComponentTypeId 31

data ConversationStatus
  = ConversationActive
  | ConversationPaused
  | ConversationArchived
  | ConversationError Text
  deriving (Show, Eq)

newtype AgentConversation = AgentConversation
  { acAgentId :: AgentId
  , acConversationId :: ConversationId
  , acRole :: AgentRole
  } deriving (Show)

instance Component AgentConversation where
  componentId _ = ComponentTypeId 32

data AgentRole = PrimaryAgent | AssistantAgent | ObserverAgent
  deriving (Show, Eq)
```

### Turn Components
```haskell
newtype TurnConfig = TurnConfig
  { turnConversationId :: ConversationId
  , turnParentTurnId :: Maybe TurnId  -- For branching
  } deriving (Show)

instance Component TurnConfig where
  componentId _ = ComponentTypeId 33

newtype TurnState = TurnState
  { turnStatus :: TurnStatus
  , turnStartedAt :: UTCTime
  , turnCompletedAt :: Maybe UTCTime
  } deriving (Show)

instance Component TurnState where
  componentId _ = ComponentTypeId 34

data TurnStatus
  = TurnStarting
  | TurnRunning
  | TurnToolCallsPending [ToolCallId]
  | TurnCompleted Text
  | TurnFailed Text
  deriving (Show, Eq)
```

### Tool Call Components
```haskell
newtype ToolCallConfig = ToolCallConfig
  { tcTurnId :: TurnId
  , tcToolName :: Text
  , tcToolInput :: Value
  , tcParentCallId :: Maybe ToolCallId  -- For nested calls
  } deriving (Show)

instance Component ToolCallConfig where
  componentId _ = ComponentTypeId 35

newtype ToolCallState = ToolCallState
  { tcStatus :: ToolCallStatus
  , tcStartedAt :: UTCTime
  , tcCompletedAt :: Maybe UTCTime
  , tcResult :: Maybe Value
  } deriving (Show)

instance Component ToolCallState where
  componentId _ = ComponentTypeId 36

data ToolCallStatus
  = TcPending
  | TcExecuting
  | TcCompleted Value
  | TcFailed Text
  | TcCancelled
  deriving (Show, Eq)
```

### Lineage Tracking
```haskell
newtype Lineage = Lineage
  { lineageStack :: [LineageFrame]
  } deriving (Show)

instance Component Lineage where
  componentId _ = ComponentTypeId 37

data LineageFrame = LineageFrame
  { frameType :: FrameType
  , frameId :: EntityId
  , frameTimestamp :: UTCTime
  } deriving (Show)

data FrameType
  = ConversationFrame
  | TurnFrame
  | ToolCallFrame
  | SystemFrame
  deriving (Show, Eq)

-- Message storage
newtype Message = Message
  { msgConversationId :: ConversationId
  , msgTurnId :: TurnId
  , msgRole :: MessageRole
  , msgContent :: Text
  , msgToolCalls :: [ToolCallId]
  , msgTimestamp :: UTCTime
  } deriving (Show)

instance Component Message where
  componentId _ = ComponentTypeId 38

data MessageRole = UserRole | AssistantRole | SystemRole | ToolRole
  deriving (Show, Eq)

-- Helper functions
buildLineageContext :: Lineage -> Map Text Value
pushLineage :: FrameType -> EntityId -> Lineage -> Lineage
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Create new module `System.Agents.OS.Conversation`
- [ ] Define conversation-related components
- [ ] Define turn-related components
- [ ] Define tool call components with parent-child relationships
- [ ] Define lineage tracking components
- [ ] Implement `buildLineageContext` for creating context maps
- [ ] Write tests for conversation lifecycle
- [ ] Write tests for lineage construction
- [ ] Write tests for conversation branching

## Tradeoffs

1. **Branching Conversations**: Tree-structured conversations add complexity but enable exploring multiple responses from the same point.

2. **Nested Tool Calls**: Maximum depth limits should be enforced to prevent infinite recursion. Default: 10 levels.

3. **Lineage Performance**: Building lineage context on every log call may be expensive. Consider lazy evaluation or caching.

4. **Message Storage**: Storing all messages in components may consume significant memory for long conversations. Consider pagination or external storage.

## Integration Points

Uses types from:
- `System.Agents.OS.Core` (EntityId, Component, AgentId, ConversationId, TurnId, ToolCallId)

Replaces/extends:
- `System.Agents.Session.Types` (Session, Turn, LlmTurnContent, UserTurnContent)
- `System.Agents.Tools.Context` (CallStackEntry, ToolExecutionContext)

## Files to Create/Modify

- Create: `src/System/Agents/OS/Conversation.hs`
- Create: `src/System/Agents/OS/Conversation/Types.hs`
- Create: `src/System/Agents/OS/Conversation/Lineage.hs`
- Create: `test/OS/ConversationTests.hs`"}

{"title": "OS Model: Core Entity and Component Types", "dependencies": "", "branch_name": "gh-os-architecture", "is_final": "false", "body": "## Overview

Define the foundational Entity-Component-System (ECS) types for the new Operating System architecture. This is the foundational issue that establishes the core data model inspired by https://dicioccio.fr/how-i-write-purescript-apps.html#the-cheap-entity-component-system.

## Background

Moving from a hierarchical Runtime-per-agent structure to a flat ECS model requires redefining how we represent agents, toolboxes, and resources. In ECS:
- **Entities** are unique identifiers (just IDs)
- **Components** are pure data attached to entities
- **Systems** are functions that process entities with specific components

## Types to Define

### Core Identifiers
```haskell
-- | Unique identifier for any entity in the system
newtype EntityId = EntityId { unEntityId :: UUID }
  deriving (Eq, Ord, Show, Hashable)

-- | Phantom-typed entity IDs for type safety
newtype AgentId = AgentId { unAgentId :: EntityId }
newtype ToolboxId = ToolboxId { unToolboxId :: EntityId }
newtype ConversationId = ConversationId { unConversationId :: EntityId }
newtype TurnId = TurnId { unTurnId :: EntityId }
newtype ToolCallId = ToolCallId { unToolCallId :: EntityId }
newtype ResourceId = ResourceId { unResourceId :: EntityId }
```

### Component System
```haskell
-- | A component is any data type that can be attached to an entity
class Component a where
  componentId :: Proxy a -> ComponentTypeId

-- | Type-level identifier for component types
newtype ComponentTypeId = ComponentTypeId { unComponentTypeId :: Int }
  deriving (Eq, Ord, Show)

-- | Storage for all components of a specific type
newtype ComponentStore a = ComponentStore
  { store :: HashMap EntityId a }

-- | The world contains all component stores
newtype World = World
  { componentStores :: HashMap ComponentTypeId (TVar Any) }
```

### Agent Components
```haskell
newtype AgentConfig = AgentConfig
  { agentName :: Text
  , agentModel :: ModelConfig
  , agentSystemPrompt :: Text
  , agentToolboxBindings :: [ToolboxBindingSpec]
  } deriving (Show, Generic)

instance Component AgentConfig where
  componentId _ = ComponentTypeId 1

newtype AgentState = AgentState
  { agentStatus :: AgentStatus
  , agentCurrentConversation :: Maybe ConversationId
  , agentCreatedAt :: UTCTime
  } deriving (Show)

instance Component AgentState where
  componentId _ = ComponentTypeId 2

data AgentStatus = AgentIdle | AgentBusy TurnId | AgentError Text
  deriving (Show, Eq)
```

### Toolbox Components
```haskell
newtype ToolboxConfig = ToolboxConfig
  { toolboxName :: Text
  , toolboxType :: ToolboxType
  , toolboxSettings :: Value
  } deriving (Show)

instance Component ToolboxConfig where
  componentId _ = ComponentTypeId 3

newtype ToolboxState = ToolboxState
  { toolboxScope :: ResourceScope
  , toolboxStatus :: ToolboxStatus
  , toolboxResourceRef :: Maybe ResourceId
  } deriving (Show)

instance Component ToolboxState where
  componentId _ = ComponentTypeId 4

newtype ToolboxBinding = ToolboxBinding
  { bindingAgentId :: AgentId
  , bindingToolboxId :: ToolboxId
  , bindingConfig :: Value
  } deriving (Show)

instance Component ToolboxBinding where
  componentId _ = ComponentTypeId 5
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Create new module `System.Agents.OS.Core`
- [ ] Define `EntityId` and phantom-typed ID wrappers
- [ ] Define `Component` typeclass with auto-generated IDs
- [ ] Define `World` type with heterogeneous component storage
- [ ] Define all agent-related components
- [ ] Define all toolbox-related components
- [ ] Add type safety tests to verify phantom types work correctly
- [ ] Document tradeoffs in module header

## Tradeoffs

1. **Type Erasure (Any) vs Type Safety**: Using `TVar Any` requires careful casting but enables heterogeneous storage. Alternative would be a closed-world sum type which limits extensibility.

2. **STM vs MVar**: STM allows composable transactions, preferred for complex resource coordination across multiple entities.

3. **Component ID Assignment**: Consider Template Haskell for auto-generating IDs to prevent collisions when adding new component types.

## Migration Notes

This module is foundational and does not interact with existing code. It establishes the new data model that will eventually replace:
- `System.Agents.Runtime.Runtime`
- `System.Agents.Base.Agent` (description part)
- Parts of `System.Agents.Tools.Base`

## Files to Create/Modify

- Create: `src/System/Agents/OS/Core.hs`
- Create: `src/System/Agents/OS/Core/Types.hs`
- Create: `src/System/Agents/OS/Core/World.hs`
- Create: `test/OS/CoreTests.hs`"}

function.arguments:
{"title": "OS Model: Resource Management and Lifecycle Scopes", "dependencies": "#1", "branch_name": "gh-os-architecture", "is_final": "false", "body": "## Overview

Define the resource management system with multiple lifecycle scopes and proper cleanup. This issue depends on the Core Entity types being defined first.

## Background

Tools require resources like:
- **SQLite**: file handles, prepared statements
- **Lua**: interpreter states
- **HTTP**: connection managers
- **Processes**: file descriptors

These resources have different lifecycles:
- **per-program**: Global HTTP connection pool
- **per-agent**: Agent-specific sandbox
- **per-toolbox**: SQLite connection per toolbox instance
- **per-conversation**: Isolated Lua state
- **per-turn**: Temporary resources
- **per-toolcall**: Single-use resources

## Types to Define

### Scope Definition
```haskell
-- | Defines the lifetime of a resource
newtype ResourceScope = ResourceScope
  { scopeHierarchy :: [ScopeLevel] }

data ScopeLevel
  = ProgramScope
  | AgentScope AgentId
  | ToolboxScope ToolboxId
  | ConversationScope ConversationId
  | TurnScope TurnId
  | ToolCallScope ToolCallId
  deriving (Eq, Ord, Show)
```

### Resource Components
```haskell
newtype ResourceInfo = ResourceInfo
  { resourceId :: ResourceId
  , resourceScope :: ResourceScope
  , resourceType :: ResourceType
  , resourceCreatedAt :: UTCTime
  , resourceLastAccessed :: TVar UTCTime
  } deriving (Show)

instance Component ResourceInfo where
  componentId _ = ComponentTypeId 10

data ResourceType
  = SqliteResource SqliteConfig
  | LuaResource LuaConfig
  | HttpManagerResource HttpManagerConfig
  | ProcessResource ProcessConfig
  deriving (Show)

data ResourceHandle = ResourceHandle
  { handleId :: ResourceId
  , handleCleanup :: IO ()
  , handleAccess :: forall a. (ResourceAccessor -> IO a) -> IO a
  }

newtype ResourceRegistry = ResourceRegistry
  { registryHandles :: TVar (HashMap ResourceId ResourceHandle)
  , registryCounter :: TVar Int
  }

-- Context for resource operations
newtype ResourceContext = ResourceContext
  { contextScope :: [ScopeLevel]
  , contextRegistry :: ResourceRegistry
  }
```

### Resource-Specific Components
```haskell
newtype SqliteResourceData = SqliteResourceData
  { sqliteConnection :: Connection
  , sqliteAccessMode :: SqliteAccessMode
  } deriving (Show)

instance Component SqliteResourceData where
  componentId _ = ComponentTypeId 11

data SqliteAccessMode = SerialAccess | ConcurrentReads
  deriving (Show, Eq)

newtype LuaResourceData = LuaResourceData
  { luaState :: Lua.State
  , luaSandboxed :: Bool
  } deriving (Show)

instance Component LuaResourceData where
  componentId _ = ComponentTypeId 12

newtype HttpResourceData = HttpResourceData
  { httpManager :: Manager
  , httpConnectionCount :: TVar Int
  } deriving (Show)

instance Component HttpResourceData where
  componentId _ = ComponentTypeId 13
```

### Operations
```haskell
createResource :: ResourceContext -> ResourceType 
               -> (ResourceId -> IO ResourceHandle) -> IO ResourceId
cleanupScope :: ResourceRegistry -> ScopeLevel -> IO ()
isResourceValid :: ResourceScope -> [ScopeLevel] -> Bool
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Create new module `System.Agents.OS.Resources`
- [ ] Define `ResourceScope` with all lifecycle levels
- [ ] Define `ResourceRegistry` for tracking all resources
- [ ] Implement `createResource` with proper scope tracking
- [ ] Implement `cleanupScope` that properly disposes all resources in a scope
- [ ] Add resource-specific components for SQLite, Lua, HTTP
- [ ] Write tests for resource lifecycle management
- [ ] Write tests for scope cleanup
- [ ] Document cleanup ordering guarantees

## Tradeoffs

1. **Explicit Cleanup vs Finalizers**: Explicit cleanup (`handleCleanup`) is used for predictable resource management. Finalizers are non-deterministic.

2. **Resource Sharing**: Shared resources use the same ResourceId. The registry tracks implicitly via cleanup - if two entities share a resource, only one should register cleanup.

3. **Scope Checking**: Runtime scope checking adds overhead but ensures correctness. Consider caching scope lookups.

## Integration Points

Uses types from:
- `System.Agents.OS.Core` (EntityId, Component, World)
- Existing: `System.Agents.Tools.SqliteToolbox` (for Connection type)
- Existing: `System.Agents.Tools.LuaToolbox` (for Lua.State type)

## Files to Create/Modify

- Create: `src/System/Agents/OS/Resources.hs`
- Create: `src/System/Agents/OS/Resources/Types.hs`
- Create: `src/System/Agents/OS/Resources/Sqlite.hs`
- Create: `src/System/Agents/OS/Resources/Lua.hs`
- Create: `src/System/Agents/OS/Resources/Http.hs`
- Create: `test/OS/ResourcesTests.hs`"}

function.arguments:
{"title": "OS Model: Concurrent Access and Serialization", "dependencies": "#2", "branch_name": "gh-os-architecture", "is_final": "false", "body": "## Overview

Design concurrent access patterns for shared resources. This builds on the Resource Management system to provide safe concurrent access to toolbox resources.

## Background

Multiple agents may need concurrent access to:
- Same SQLite database (readers can be concurrent, writers must be exclusive)
- HTTP connection pools (can be shared across concurrent requests)
- Lua interpreters (typically exclusive access per execution)
- Process handles (exclusive per process)

The current implementation uses MVars within each toolbox. The new system should use STM for composability and support multiple access patterns.

## Types to Define

### Access Patterns
```haskell
-- | Access pattern for a resource
data AccessPattern
  = ExclusiveAccess      -- Only one accessor (MVar-like)
  | ReadWriteAccess      -- Multiple readers, single writer
  | PoolAccess Int       -- Connection pool with max size
  | StatelessAccess      -- No synchronization needed

-- | Resource access control configuration
newtype AccessControl = AccessControl
  { accessPattern :: AccessPattern
  , accessTimeout :: Maybe NominalDiffTime
  } deriving (Show)

instance Component AccessControl where
  componentId _ = ComponentTypeId 20
```

### Synchronization Primitives
```haskell
-- STM-based read-write lock
data RWLock = RWLock
  { rwLockReaders :: TVar Int
  , rwLockWriter :: TVar Bool
  , rwLockWaitQueue :: TVar [TMVar ()]
  }

-- Resource synchronization state
newtype ResourceSync = ResourceSync
  { syncLock :: SyncPrimitive
  } deriving (Show)

instance Component ResourceSync where
  componentId _ = ComponentTypeId 21

data SyncPrimitive
  = ExclusiveLock (TMVar ())
  | ReadWriteLock RWLock
  | PoolLock (TBQueue ResourceToken)
  | NoLock
  deriving (Show)

data ResourceToken = ResourceToken
  { tokenResource :: ResourceId
  , tokenRelease :: STM ()
  }
```

### Resource Access Monad
```haskell
newtype ResourceM a = ResourceM
  { unResourceM :: ReaderT ResourceContext (ExceptT ResourceError IO) a }

data ResourceError
  = ResourceNotFound ResourceId
  | ResourceAccessTimeout ResourceId NominalDiffTime
  | ResourceScopeInvalid ResourceId [ScopeLevel]
  | ResourceClosed ResourceId
  | ResourceBusy ResourceId
  deriving (Show, Eq)

-- Operations
withExclusive :: ResourceId -> ResourceM a -> ResourceM a
withRead :: ResourceId -> ResourceM a -> ResourceM a
withWrite :: ResourceId -> ResourceM a -> ResourceM a
withPooled :: ResourceId -> ResourceM a -> ResourceM a
```

### Resource-Specific Access Control
```haskell
newtype SqliteAccess = SqliteAccess
  { sqliteRwLock :: RWLock
  , sqliteWalMode :: Bool
  } deriving (Show)

instance Component SqliteAccess where
  componentId _ = ComponentTypeId 22

newtype LuaAccess = LuaAccess
  { luaLock :: TMVar () } deriving (Show)

instance Component LuaAccess where
  componentId _ = ComponentTypeId 23

newtype HttpAccess = HttpAccess
  { httpPool :: TBQueue ResourceToken
  , httpManagerRef :: ResourceId
  } deriving (Show)

instance Component HttpAccess where
  componentId _ = ComponentTypeId 24
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Create new module `System.Agents.OS.Concurrent`
- [ ] Implement STM-based RWLock with reader/writer semantics
- [ ] Implement `withExclusive` using TMVar
- [ ] Implement `withRead`/`withWrite` using RWLock
- [ ] Implement `withPooled` using TBQueue
- [ ] Add resource-specific access components for SQLite, Lua, HTTP
- [ ] Write concurrency tests with multiple threads
- [ ] Write stress tests for resource contention
- [ ] Document thread-safety guarantees

## Tradeoffs

1. **STM vs MVar**: STM chosen for composable transactions and automatic retry on conflict. MVar is slightly faster but less composable.

2. **Fairness**: Current RWLock design can starve writers. Consider writer-priority variant if contention becomes an issue.

3. **Pool Sizing**: Static pool sizes are simpler but may waste resources. Dynamic sizing could be added later.

4. **Timeout Handling**: Optional timeouts allow callers to decide between waiting and failing fast.

## Integration Points

Uses types from:
- `System.Agents.OS.Core` (EntityId, Component)
- `System.Agents.OS.Resources` (ResourceId, ResourceContext, ResourceRegistry)

## Files to Create/Modify

- Create: `src/System/Agents/OS/Concurrent.hs`
- Create: `src/System/Agents/OS/Concurrent/Types.hs`
- Create: `src/System/Agents/OS/Concurrent/Locks.hs`
- Create: `test/OS/ConcurrentTests.hs`"}

function.arguments:
{"title": "OS Model: Conversation and Lineage Tracking", "dependencies": "#1", "branch_name": "gh-os-architecture", "is_final": "false", "body": "## Overview

Define types for conversation/thread tracking and lineage tracking for nested tool calls. This enables thorough tracing of agent interactions and tool call hierarchies.

## Background

The system needs to track:
- **Conversations**: Top-level interactions between users and agents
- **Turns**: Individual steps within a conversation
- **Tool Calls**: Individual tool invocations, potentially nested
- **Lineage**: Full call chain for debugging and accounting

This supports:
- Recursive/nested agent calls with proper depth tracking
- Conversation forking (exploring multiple responses)
- Complete audit trails for debugging

## Types to Define

### Conversation Components
```haskell
newtype ConversationConfig = ConversationConfig
  { conversationTitle :: Maybe Text
  , conversationMetadata :: Map Text Value
  } deriving (Show)

instance Component ConversationConfig where
  componentId _ = ComponentTypeId 30

newtype ConversationState = ConversationState
  { conversationAgentId :: AgentId
  , conversationStatus :: ConversationStatus
  , conversationStartedAt :: UTCTime
  , conversationLastActivity :: TVar UTCTime
  } deriving (Show)

instance Component ConversationState where
  componentId _ = ComponentTypeId 31

data ConversationStatus
  = ConversationActive
  | ConversationPaused
  | ConversationArchived
  | ConversationError Text
  deriving (Show, Eq)

newtype AgentConversation = AgentConversation
  { acAgentId :: AgentId
  , acConversationId :: ConversationId
  , acRole :: AgentRole
  } deriving (Show)

instance Component AgentConversation where
  componentId _ = ComponentTypeId 32

data AgentRole = PrimaryAgent | AssistantAgent | ObserverAgent
  deriving (Show, Eq)
```

### Turn Components
```haskell
newtype TurnConfig = TurnConfig
  { turnConversationId :: ConversationId
  , turnParentTurnId :: Maybe TurnId  -- For branching
  } deriving (Show)

instance Component TurnConfig where
  componentId _ = ComponentTypeId 33

newtype TurnState = TurnState
  { turnStatus :: TurnStatus
  , turnStartedAt :: UTCTime
  , turnCompletedAt :: Maybe UTCTime
  } deriving (Show)

instance Component TurnState where
  componentId _ = ComponentTypeId 34

data TurnStatus
  = TurnStarting
  | TurnRunning
  | TurnToolCallsPending [ToolCallId]
  | TurnCompleted Text
  | TurnFailed Text
  deriving (Show, Eq)
```

### Tool Call Components
```haskell
newtype ToolCallConfig = ToolCallConfig
  { tcTurnId :: TurnId
  , tcToolName :: Text
  , tcToolInput :: Value
  , tcParentCallId :: Maybe ToolCallId  -- For nested calls
  } deriving (Show)

instance Component ToolCallConfig where
  componentId _ = ComponentTypeId 35

newtype ToolCallState = ToolCallState
  { tcStatus :: ToolCallStatus
  , tcStartedAt :: UTCTime
  , tcCompletedAt :: Maybe UTCTime
  , tcResult :: Maybe Value
  } deriving (Show)

instance Component ToolCallState where
  componentId _ = ComponentTypeId 36

data ToolCallStatus
  = TcPending
  | TcExecuting
  | TcCompleted Value
  | TcFailed Text
  | TcCancelled
  deriving (Show, Eq)
```

### Lineage Tracking
```haskell
newtype Lineage = Lineage
  { lineageStack :: [LineageFrame]
  } deriving (Show)

instance Component Lineage where
  componentId _ = ComponentTypeId 37

data LineageFrame = LineageFrame
  { frameType :: FrameType
  , frameId :: EntityId
  , frameTimestamp :: UTCTime
  } deriving (Show)

data FrameType
  = ConversationFrame
  | TurnFrame
  | ToolCallFrame
  | SystemFrame
  deriving (Show, Eq)

-- Message storage
newtype Message = Message
  { msgConversationId :: ConversationId
  , msgTurnId :: TurnId
  , msgRole :: MessageRole
  , msgContent :: Text
  , msgToolCalls :: [ToolCallId]
  , msgTimestamp :: UTCTime
  } deriving (Show)

instance Component Message where
  componentId _ = ComponentTypeId 38

data MessageRole = UserRole | AssistantRole | SystemRole | ToolRole
  deriving (Show, Eq)

-- Helper functions
buildLineageContext :: Lineage -> Map Text Value
pushLineage :: FrameType -> EntityId -> Lineage -> Lineage
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Create new module `System.Agents.OS.Conversation`
- [ ] Define conversation-related components
- [ ] Define turn-related components
- [ ] Define tool call components with parent-child relationships
- [ ] Define lineage tracking components
- [ ] Implement `buildLineageContext` for creating context maps
- [ ] Write tests for conversation lifecycle
- [ ] Write tests for lineage construction
- [ ] Write tests for conversation branching

## Tradeoffs

1. **Branching Conversations**: Tree-structured conversations add complexity but enable exploring multiple responses from the same point.

2. **Nested Tool Calls**: Maximum depth limits should be enforced to prevent infinite recursion. Default: 10 levels.

3. **Lineage Performance**: Building lineage context on every log call may be expensive. Consider lazy evaluation or caching.

4. **Message Storage**: Storing all messages in components may consume significant memory for long conversations. Consider pagination or external storage.

## Integration Points

Uses types from:
- `System.Agents.OS.Core` (EntityId, Component, AgentId, ConversationId, TurnId, ToolCallId)

Replaces/extends:
- `System.Agents.Session.Types` (Session, Turn, LlmTurnContent, UserTurnContent)
- `System.Agents.Tools.Context` (CallStackEntry, ToolExecutionContext)

## Files to Create/Modify

- Create: `src/System/Agents/OS/Conversation.hs`
- Create: `src/System/Agents/OS/Conversation/Types.hs`
- Create: `src/System/Agents/OS/Conversation/Lineage.hs`
- Create: `test/OS/ConversationTests.hs`"}

{"title": "OS Model: Core Entity and Component Types", "dependencies": "", "branch_name": "gh-os-architecture", "is_final": "false", "body": "## Overview

Define the foundational Entity-Component-System (ECS) types for the new Operating System architecture. This is the foundational issue that establishes the core data model inspired by https://dicioccio.fr/how-i-write-purescript-apps.html#the-cheap-entity-component-system.

## Background

Moving from a hierarchical Runtime-per-agent structure to a flat ECS model requires redefining how we represent agents, toolboxes, and resources. In ECS:
- **Entities** are unique identifiers (just IDs)
- **Components** are pure data attached to entities
- **Systems** are functions that process entities with specific components

## Types to Define

### Core Identifiers
```haskell
-- | Unique identifier for any entity in the system
newtype EntityId = EntityId { unEntityId :: UUID }
  deriving (Eq, Ord, Show, Hashable)

-- | Phantom-typed entity IDs for type safety
newtype AgentId = AgentId { unAgentId :: EntityId }
newtype ToolboxId = ToolboxId { unToolboxId :: EntityId }
newtype ConversationId = ConversationId { unConversationId :: EntityId }
newtype TurnId = TurnId { unTurnId :: EntityId }
newtype ToolCallId = ToolCallId { unToolCallId :: EntityId }
newtype ResourceId = ResourceId { unResourceId :: EntityId }
```

### Component System
```haskell
-- | A component is any data type that can be attached to an entity
class Component a where
  componentId :: Proxy a -> ComponentTypeId

-- | Type-level identifier for component types
newtype ComponentTypeId = ComponentTypeId { unComponentTypeId :: Int }
  deriving (Eq, Ord, Show)

-- | Storage for all components of a specific type
newtype ComponentStore a = ComponentStore
  { store :: HashMap EntityId a }

-- | The world contains all component stores
newtype World = World
  { componentStores :: HashMap ComponentTypeId (TVar Any) }
```

### Agent Components
```haskell
newtype AgentConfig = AgentConfig
  { agentName :: Text
  , agentModel :: ModelConfig
  , agentSystemPrompt :: Text
  , agentToolboxBindings :: [ToolboxBindingSpec]
  } deriving (Show, Generic)

instance Component AgentConfig where
  componentId _ = ComponentTypeId 1

newtype AgentState = AgentState
  { agentStatus :: AgentStatus
  , agentCurrentConversation :: Maybe ConversationId
  , agentCreatedAt :: UTCTime
  } deriving (Show)

instance Component AgentState where
  componentId _ = ComponentTypeId 2

data AgentStatus = AgentIdle | AgentBusy TurnId | AgentError Text
  deriving (Show, Eq)
```

### Toolbox Components
```haskell
newtype ToolboxConfig = ToolboxConfig
  { toolboxName :: Text
  , toolboxType :: ToolboxType
  , toolboxSettings :: Value
  } deriving (Show)

instance Component ToolboxConfig where
  componentId _ = ComponentTypeId 3

newtype ToolboxState = ToolboxState
  { toolboxScope :: ResourceScope
  , toolboxStatus :: ToolboxStatus
  , toolboxResourceRef :: Maybe ResourceId
  } deriving (Show)

instance Component ToolboxState where
  componentId _ = ComponentTypeId 4

newtype ToolboxBinding = ToolboxBinding
  { bindingAgentId :: AgentId
  , bindingToolboxId :: ToolboxId
  , bindingConfig :: Value
  } deriving (Show)

instance Component ToolboxBinding where
  componentId _ = ComponentTypeId 5
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Create new module `System.Agents.OS.Core`
- [ ] Define `EntityId` and phantom-typed ID wrappers
- [ ] Define `Component` typeclass with auto-generated IDs
- [ ] Define `World` type with heterogeneous component storage
- [ ] Define all agent-related components
- [ ] Define all toolbox-related components
- [ ] Add type safety tests to verify phantom types work correctly
- [ ] Document tradeoffs in module header

## Tradeoffs

1. **Type Erasure (Any) vs Type Safety**: Using `TVar Any` requires careful casting but enables heterogeneous storage. Alternative would be a closed-world sum type which limits extensibility.

2. **STM vs MVar**: STM allows composable transactions, preferred for complex resource coordination across multiple entities.

3. **Component ID Assignment**: Consider Template Haskell for auto-generating IDs to prevent collisions when adding new component types.

## Migration Notes

This module is foundational and does not interact with existing code. It establishes the new data model that will eventually replace:
- `System.Agents.Runtime.Runtime`
- `System.Agents.Base.Agent` (description part)
- Parts of `System.Agents.Tools.Base`

## Files to Create/Modify

- Create: `src/System/Agents/OS/Core.hs`
- Create: `src/System/Agents/OS/Core/Types.hs`
- Create: `src/System/Agents/OS/Core/World.hs`
- Create: `test/OS/CoreTests.hs`"}

{"title": "OS Model: Concurrent Access and Serialization", "dependencies": "#2", "branch_name": "gh-os-architecture", "is_final": "false", "body": "## Overview

data AccessPattern
  = ExclusiveAccess      -- Only one accessor (MVar-like)

-- | Resource access control configuration

### Synchronization Primitives
```haskell
-- STM-based read-write lock
data RWLock = RWLock
  { rwLockReaders :: TVar Int
  , rwLockWriter :: TVar Bool
  , rwLockWaitQueue :: TVar [TMVar ()]
  }

-- Resource synchronization state
newtype ResourceSync = ResourceSync
  { syncLock :: SyncPrimitive
  } deriving (Show)

instance Component ResourceSync where
  componentId _ = ComponentTypeId 21

data SyncPrimitive
  = ExclusiveLock (TMVar ())
  | ReadWriteLock RWLock
  | PoolLock (TBQueue ResourceToken)
  | NoLock
  deriving (Show)

data ResourceToken = ResourceToken
  { tokenResource :: ResourceId
  , tokenRelease :: STM ()
  }
```

### Resource Access Monad
```haskell
newtype ResourceM a = ResourceM
  { unResourceM :: ReaderT ResourceContext (ExceptT ResourceError IO) a }

data ResourceError
  = ResourceNotFound ResourceId
  | ResourceAccessTimeout ResourceId NominalDiffTime
  | ResourceScopeInvalid ResourceId [ScopeLevel]
  | ResourceClosed ResourceId
  | ResourceBusy ResourceId
  deriving (Show, Eq)

-- Operations
withExclusive :: ResourceId -> ResourceM a -> ResourceM a
withRead :: ResourceId -> ResourceM a -> ResourceM a
withWrite :: ResourceId -> ResourceM a -> ResourceM a
withPooled :: ResourceId -> ResourceM a -> ResourceM a
```

### Resource-Specific Access Control
```haskell
newtype SqliteAccess = SqliteAccess
  { sqliteRwLock :: RWLock
  , sqliteWalMode :: Bool
  } deriving (Show)

instance Component SqliteAccess where
  componentId _ = ComponentTypeId 22

newtype LuaAccess = LuaAccess
  { luaLock :: TMVar () } deriving (Show)

instance Component LuaAccess where
  componentId _ = ComponentTypeId 23

newtype HttpAccess = HttpAccess
  { httpPool :: TBQueue ResourceToken
  , httpManagerRef :: ResourceId
  } deriving (Show)

instance Component HttpAccess where
  componentId _ = ComponentTypeId 24
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Create new module `System.Agents.OS.Concurrent`
- [ ] Implement STM-based RWLock with reader/writer semantics
- [ ] Implement `withExclusive` using TMVar
- [ ] Implement `withRead`/`withWrite` using RWLock
- [ ] Implement `withPooled` using TBQueue
- [ ] Add resource-specific access components for SQLite, Lua, HTTP
- [ ] Write concurrency tests with multiple threads
- [ ] Write stress tests for resource contention
- [ ] Document thread-safety guarantees

## Tradeoffs

1. **STM vs MVar**: STM chosen for composable transactions and automatic retry on conflict. MVar is slightly faster but less composable.

2. **Fairness**: Current RWLock design can starve writers. Consider writer-priority variant if contention becomes an issue.

3. **Pool Sizing**: Static pool sizes are simpler but may waste resources. Dynamic sizing could be added later.

4. **Timeout Handling**: Optional timeouts allow callers to decide between waiting and failing fast.

## Integration Points

Uses types from:
- `System.Agents.OS.Core` (EntityId, Component)
- `System.Agents.OS.Resources` (ResourceId, ResourceContext, ResourceRegistry)

## Files to Create/Modify

- Create: `src/System/Agents/OS/Concurrent.hs`
- Create: `src/System/Agents/OS/Concurrent/Types.hs`
- Create: `src/System/Agents/OS/Concurrent/Locks.hs`
- Create: `test/OS/ConcurrentTests.hs`"}

function.arguments:
{"title": "OS Model: Conversation and Lineage Tracking", "dependencies": "#1", "branch_name": "gh-os-architecture", "is_final": "false", "body": "## Overview

Define types for conversation/thread tracking and lineage tracking for nested tool calls. This enables thorough tracing of agent interactions and tool call hierarchies.

## Background

The system needs to track:
- **Conversations**: Top-level interactions between users and agents
- **Turns**: Individual steps within a conversation
- **Tool Calls**: Individual tool invocations, potentially nested
- **Lineage**: Full call chain for debugging and accounting

This supports:
- Recursive/nested agent calls with proper depth tracking
- Conversation forking (exploring multiple responses)
- Complete audit trails for debugging

## Types to Define

### Conversation Components
```haskell
newtype ConversationConfig = ConversationConfig
  { conversationTitle :: Maybe Text
  , conversationMetadata :: Map Text Value
  } deriving (Show)

instance Component ConversationConfig where
  componentId _ = ComponentTypeId 30

newtype ConversationState = ConversationState
  { conversationAgentId :: AgentId
  , conversationStatus :: ConversationStatus
  , conversationStartedAt :: UTCTime
  , conversationLastActivity :: TVar UTCTime
  } deriving (Show)

instance Component ConversationState where
  componentId _ = ComponentTypeId 31

data ConversationStatus
  = ConversationActive
  | ConversationPaused
  | ConversationArchived
  | ConversationError Text
  deriving (Show, Eq)

newtype AgentConversation = AgentConversation
  { acAgentId :: AgentId
  , acConversationId :: ConversationId
  , acRole :: AgentRole
  } deriving (Show)

instance Component AgentConversation where
  componentId _ = ComponentTypeId 32

data AgentRole = PrimaryAgent | AssistantAgent | ObserverAgent
  deriving (Show, Eq)
```

### Turn Components
```haskell
newtype TurnConfig = TurnConfig
  { turnConversationId :: ConversationId
  , turnParentTurnId :: Maybe TurnId  -- For branching
  } deriving (Show)

instance Component TurnConfig where
  componentId _ = ComponentTypeId 33

newtype TurnState = TurnState
  { turnStatus :: TurnStatus
  , turnStartedAt :: UTCTime
  , turnCompletedAt :: Maybe UTCTime
  } deriving (Show)

instance Component TurnState where
  componentId _ = ComponentTypeId 34

data TurnStatus
  = TurnStarting
  | TurnRunning
  | TurnToolCallsPending [ToolCallId]
  | TurnCompleted Text
  | TurnFailed Text
  deriving (Show, Eq)
```

### Tool Call Components
```haskell
newtype ToolCallConfig = ToolCallConfig
  { tcTurnId :: TurnId
  , tcToolName :: Text
  , tcToolInput :: Value
  , tcParentCallId :: Maybe ToolCallId  -- For nested calls
  } deriving (Show)

instance Component ToolCallConfig where
  componentId _ = ComponentTypeId 35

newtype ToolCallState = ToolCallState
  { tcStatus :: ToolCallStatus
  , tcStartedAt :: UTCTime
  , tcCompletedAt :: Maybe UTCTime
  , tcResult :: Maybe Value
  } deriving (Show)

instance Component ToolCallState where
  componentId _ = ComponentTypeId 36

data ToolCallStatus
  = TcPending
  | TcExecuting
  | TcCompleted Value
  | TcFailed Text
  | TcCancelled
  deriving (Show, Eq)
```

### Lineage Tracking
```haskell
newtype Lineage = Lineage
  { lineageStack :: [LineageFrame]
  } deriving (Show)

instance Component Lineage where
  componentId _ = ComponentTypeId 37

data LineageFrame = LineageFrame
  { frameType :: FrameType
  , frameId :: EntityId
  , frameTimestamp :: UTCTime
  } deriving (Show)

data FrameType
  = ConversationFrame
  | TurnFrame
  | ToolCallFrame
  | SystemFrame
  deriving (Show, Eq)

-- Message storage
newtype Message = Message
  { msgConversationId :: ConversationId
  , msgTurnId :: TurnId
  , msgRole :: MessageRole
  , msgContent :: Text
  , msgToolCalls :: [ToolCallId]
  , msgTimestamp :: UTCTime
  } deriving (Show)

instance Component Message where
  componentId _ = ComponentTypeId 38

data MessageRole = UserRole | AssistantRole | SystemRole | ToolRole
  deriving (Show, Eq)

-- Helper functions
buildLineageContext :: Lineage -> Map Text Value
pushLineage :: FrameType -> EntityId -> Lineage -> Lineage
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Create new module `System.Agents.OS.Conversation`
- [ ] Define conversation-related components
- [ ] Define turn-related components
- [ ] Define tool call components with parent-child relationships
- [ ] Define lineage tracking components
- [ ] Implement `buildLineageContext` for creating context maps
- [ ] Write tests for conversation lifecycle
- [ ] Write tests for lineage construction
- [ ] Write tests for conversation branching

## Tradeoffs

1. **Branching Conversations**: Tree-structured conversations add complexity but enable exploring multiple responses from the same point.

2. **Nested Tool Calls**: Maximum depth limits should be enforced to prevent infinite recursion. Default: 10 levels.

3. **Lineage Performance**: Building lineage context on every log call may be expensive. Consider lazy evaluation or caching.

4. **Message Storage**: Storing all messages in components may consume significant memory for long conversations. Consider pagination or external storage.

## Integration Points

Uses types from:
- `System.Agents.OS.Core` (EntityId, Component, AgentId, ConversationId, TurnId, ToolCallId)

Replaces/extends:
- `System.Agents.Session.Types` (Session, Turn, LlmTurnContent, UserTurnContent)
- `System.Agents.Tools.Context` (CallStackEntry, ToolExecutionContext)

## Files to Create/Modify

- Create: `src/System/Agents/OS/Conversation.hs`
- Create: `src/System/Agents/OS/Conversation/Types.hs`
- Create: `src/System/Agents/OS/Conversation/Lineage.hs`
- Create: `test/OS/ConversationTests.hs`"}
